tasks.subject: [persist-per-session-settings] [P1] Add 5 nullable session-settings columns + Drizzle migration
tasks.description:
Extend the existing `session_metadata` table (ADR-0255) with five nullable per-session settings columns so settings can survive idle eviction and server restart.

FILE: `packages/db/src/schema/sessions.ts`. Add the `integer` import and the five columns. Replace the current table definition so it reads exactly:

```ts
import { sqliteTable, text, integer } from 'drizzle-orm/sqlite-core';

export const sessionMetadata = sqliteTable('session_metadata', {
  // --- Immutable identity (first-write-wins; ADR-0255) ---
  sessionId: text('session_id').primaryKey(),
  runtime: text('runtime').notNull(),
  agentPath: text('agent_path'),
  createdAt: text('created_at').notNull(),
  // --- Mutable per-session settings (last-write-wins; ADR-0260) ---
  // NULL = "no explicit preference; use the runtime's default."
  permissionMode: text('permission_mode'),
  model: text('model'),
  effort: text('effort'),
  fastMode: integer('fast_mode', { mode: 'boolean' }),
  autoMode: integer('auto_mode', { mode: 'boolean' }),
});
```

Keep the existing `SessionMetadata` / `NewSessionMetadata` inferred type exports and the existing comment about `createdAt` being ISO 8601 text. Preserve the column-group comments verbatim (immutable identity vs mutable settings; `NULL = runtime default`).

GENERATE THE MIGRATION: run `pnpm --filter @dorkos/db db:generate`. This emits a new `packages/db/drizzle/0015_*.sql` containing `ALTER TABLE "session_metadata" ADD COLUMN ...` statements for all five columns (existing migrations run 0000 through 0014). Commit the generated `0015_*.sql` and the updated `packages/db/drizzle/meta/` snapshot files.

Migrations auto-apply at boot via `migrate(db, { migrationsFolder })` in `runMigrations()` (`packages/db/src/index.ts:41-44`); no manual apply step.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All five columns are nullable, so the migration is purely additive (`ADD COLUMN`) and backward-compatible â€” existing rows get `NULL` (= runtime default), no backfill.
- `pnpm --filter @dorkos/db db:check` passes (the CI gate fails if generated SQL is uncommitted), confirming the schema and generated SQL are in sync.
- A fresh DB boot creates the columns; an existing DB boot applies the `ALTER`s without data loss.
- `pnpm typecheck` passes.
VERIFY: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/db db:generate && pnpm --filter @dorkos/db db:check && pnpm typecheck`.

tasks.subject: [persist-per-session-settings] [P3] Full test suite: hydration, write-through, overlay, flag-inertness, instant switch
tasks.description:
Author the full Vitest suite from the spec's Testing Strategy, each test with a purpose comment. Some store-only cases are covered by task 2.1; this task covers the remaining hydration/write-through/overlay/regression cases and confirms the whole feature green.

TEST CASES (use `vi.mock` where needed; real in-memory `createDb(':memory:')` + `runMigrations` for store-backed cases; `FakeAgentRuntime`/`collectSseEvents` from `@dorkos/test-utils` and SDK scenario builders in `services/runtimes/claude-code/__tests__/sdk-scenarios.ts` where appropriate):

- RuntimeRegistry settings store (`apps/server/src/services/core/__tests__/runtime-registry.test.ts`): `saveSessionSettings` UPSERT creates a row with inferred runtime when none exists; updates only provided columns and leaves `runtime`/`agentPath` intact on conflict; `getSessionSettings` maps `NULL`->omitted; round-trips booleans correctly; `getSessionSettingsMany` batch read. (Extends what task 2.1 wrote â€” ensure all listed cases exist.)
- Hydration (`session-store` / `claude-code-runtime` tests): cold `ensureForMessage` with a persisted `bypassPermissions` row -> the in-memory session is `bypassPermissions` (regression test for the reported bug â€” set bypass, evict/recreate, assert hydrated bypass); precedence `opts override > persisted > runtime default`; no persisted row -> runtime default (`'default'`); all five settings hydrate, not just `permissionMode`.
- Write-through: `updateSession` calls `saveSessionSettings` with exactly the changed fields; a per-send `MessageOpts.permissionMode` override does NOT trigger a write (assert `saveSessionSettings` is not called on the send path).
- Display overlay (`sessions` route tests): `GET /:id` and `GET /` return store values over transcript-derived ones; a legacy session with no row falls back to the transcript without error.
- Cross-runtime: a runtime that does not implement `setSessionSettings` still functions (the port is optional â€” assert no crash when the setter is absent); a stored mode not in the resolved runtime's capability `values` falls back to that runtime's `default`.
- Flag-inertness regression guard (ADR-0261): with `allowDangerouslySkipPermissions: true` always set, `default` mode still routes to `canUseTool` and a `deny` still blocks the tool â€” assert the message-sender sets the flag unconditionally, and assert `canUseTool` gating is unchanged. This guards against a future SDK version making the flag non-inert.
- Instant live switch: switching an active session to bypass persists the mode and does NOT 422 or revert; a failed live `setPermissionMode` is swallowed (mode still persisted for the next turn â€” assert no throw and the in-memory mode remains the new value).
- Migration: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/db db:check` passes (generated SQL committed); fresh-DB boot creates the columns; existing-DB boot applies the `ALTER`s without data loss.

Follow `.claude/rules/testing.md`: tests in `__tests__/`, `vi.clearAllMocks()` in `beforeEach`, no arbitrary timeouts, test behavior not implementation, restore any `console`/spy mocks in `afterEach`. SDK-level scenario builders must stay inside the `services/runtimes/claude-code/` ESLint boundary.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: every case above exists with a purpose comment and passes. Full repo gates green.
VERIFY: `pnpm test -- --run && pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm --filter @dorkos/db db:check`.

tasks.subject: [persist-per-session-settings] [P3] Manual reproduction of both fixed bugs
tasks.description:
Manually reproduce both original bugs end-to-end and confirm they are fixed, using a live dev server (`pnpm dev` or `pnpm dev:dogfood`) and the testing agent / browser.

REPRO 1 â€” eviction/restart durability (the reported bug): In the UI, set a session's permission mode to `bypassPermissions` (badge shows "Permissions bypassed"). Then either wait past the 30-minute idle eviction (`SESSIONS.TIMEOUT_MS`) OR restart the dev server (faster â€” wipes the in-memory warm cache). Send a message that writes a file. CONFIRM: NO approval prompt appears (the runtime hydrated `bypassPermissions` from `session_metadata` on the cold path), and the badge/toolbar still show bypass and agree with what the agent enforces.

REPRO 2 â€” instant live switch (ADR-0261): Start an actively-running session in a non-bypass mode, then switch it to `bypassPermissions` mid-run via the permission-mode dropdown. CONFIRM: no 422 error and no optimistic-UI revert; bypass takes effect for the rest of the current turn (the live `setPermissionMode` succeeds because the query was launched with `allowDangerouslySkipPermissions`).

Optionally inspect the SQLite `session_metadata` row (e.g. via `apps/server/.temp/.dork/` in dev or `~/.dork/` in dogfood) to confirm `permission_mode='bypassPermissions'` is persisted.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: both repros confirm the fixed behavior (no prompt after eviction/restart; no 422/revert on live switch). Record the observed outcome.
VERIFY: manual â€” drive the live app and observe; confirm the persisted DB row when in doubt.
tasks.activeForm: Manually reproducing and confirming both bug fixes

tasks.subject: [persist-per-session-settings] [P4] TSDoc, contributing guide note, and flip ADR-0260/0261 to accepted
tasks.description:
Document the new contract and flip the two ADRs to accepted once implementation is verified.

TSDOC (verify present / add): `SessionSettingsPort` (in `packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts` â€” already added in 1.2); the new `RuntimeRegistry` methods `getSessionSettings`/`saveSessionSettings`/`getSessionSettingsMany` (in `apps/server/src/services/core/runtime-registry.ts` â€” added in 2.1); the schema column groups in `packages/db/src/schema/sessions.ts` documenting immutable identity (first-write-wins; ADR-0255) vs mutable settings (last-write-wins; ADR-0260) and that `NULL = runtime default` (the group comments from task 1.1 satisfy this â€” confirm they read clearly). TSDoc is lint-enforced (`eslint-plugin-jsdoc`) on exports.

CONTRIBUTING GUIDE: add the "core owns session-settings persistence via a narrow port" pattern to `contributing/architecture.md` (or the runtime/session guide if one is a better fit â€” check `contributing/INDEX.md`). Describe: persisted store = source of truth, in-memory `AgentSession` = warm cache, SDK transcript = legacy fallback; runtimes hydrate in `ensureForMessage` and write through in `updateSession` via the injected `SessionSettingsPort`; display overlay lives in the route. Aim it at future runtime authors (e.g. Codex) so they inherit durable settings by accepting one injected port. Follow `writing-developer-guides` conventions.

ADRs: flip `decisions/0260-persist-per-session-settings-via-narrow-core-port.md` and `decisions/0261-always-launch-with-dangerously-skip-permissions-for-instant-mode-switching.md` from `proposed` to `accepted` (use `/adr:review`), and update `decisions/manifest.json` accordingly. Only flip after tests (3.1) and manual repro (3.2) confirm the behavior.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: TSDoc present on the listed exports (lint clean); the contributing guide has the narrow-port pattern section; both ADRs are `accepted` in their frontmatter and in `decisions/manifest.json`.
VERIFY: `pnpm lint` (TSDoc) and `grep -c accepted decisions/0260-*.md decisions/0261-*.md`.
tasks.activeForm: Documenting the port pattern and accepting the ADRs
